meaning: n.[音]对位法,旋律配合,对应物
examples: The two melodies are played in counterpoint.
This work is in austere counterpoint to that of Gaudi.
Paris is just a short train journey away, providing the perfect counterpoint to the peace and quiet of Reims.
Another counterpoint: Intel announced it'd sell its own cheap desktop PC in developing countries.
And that will afford him the opportunity to discuss the issues in a point-counterpoint way.
